Today I Deconstruct Deconstruction

2 min read · tweet / X post · 2026-05-18
deconstructionfrench-theoryfoucaultderridadeleuzecultural-criticismpostmodernismintellectual-historycivilizationideology
Source: https://x.com/brivael/status/2055414755435889086?s=46&t=OHfXTbjdV4tiD3nnG1lsTw

Summary

Brivael Le Pogam presents a polemical thread arguing that deconstruction is the most effective 'mental virus' ever engineered against a civilization. He traces its origin to three French thinkers — Foucault, Derrida, and Deleuze — who developed its core ideas between 1966 and 1980. The framing positions deconstruction not as a neutral philosophical method but as a deliberately corrosive intellectual weapon. The thread claims this body of thought was exported to the United States, where it was hybridized with other currents (the excerpt cuts off, but the implication is identity politics, critical theory, or related frameworks). This narrative echoes a well-established conservative and reactionary critique that 'French Theory' mutated in American academia into the ideological engine behind contemporary progressive movements, including wokeness and cancel culture. Le Pogam's approach is itself a rhetorical act of deconstruction — turning the method against itself — which makes the thread self-consciously provocative. While the argument compresses decades of complex intellectual history into a viral-ready format, it reflects a growing populist backlash against postmodern philosophy and its perceived downstream cultural effects in Western societies.

Key Takeaways

  • The author frames deconstruction as a consciously designed ideological weapon against Western civilization, not merely an academic philosophy.
  • Foucault, Derrida, and Deleuze are identified as the three architects of deconstruction between 1966 and 1980.
  • The thread follows the 'French Theory export' thesis: ideas born in Paris were transplanted into American universities and mutated into broader cultural movements.
  • The hybridization claim suggests deconstruction merged with American identity politics or critical race theory to gain mainstream influence.
  • The rhetorical strategy of 'deconstructing deconstruction' is deliberately provocative and mirrors the very method it critiques.
  • This critique belongs to a broader trend of anti-postmodernist backlash visible in figures like Jordan Peterson, James Lindsay, and others.
  • The tweet format condenses a complex intellectual genealogy into a memetic, shareable narrative — itself an example of how ideas propagate virally.

Full Text / Transcript

Aujourd'hui je déconstruis la déconstruction. La déconstruction est le virus mental le plus efficace jamais conçu contre une civilisation. Il a été fabriqué en France entre 1966 et 1980 par trois hommes : Foucault, Derrida, Deleuze. Il a été exporté aux États-Unis, hybridé avec
